subject: Project Management
title: The FBI Virtual Case File
description: A micro-lesson that makes The FBI Virtual Case File usable.

hook.text: The FBI spent $170 million on Virtual Case File — a waterfall project so bad it was scrapped entirely.

buildup.text: Started in 2001 to modernize the FBI's case management. Requirements were gathered for years before coding began.

discovery.text: By the time development finished, the requirements were obsolete. The software didn't match how agents worked.

twist.text: The project had 800 pages of requirements but almost no user feedback during development. Classic waterfall mistake.

climax.text: In 2005, the FBI abandoned VCF and started over with Sentinel — which also struggled until they adopted agile.

punchline.text: 800 pages of requirements. Zero working software. $170 million gone.

quiz.question: Why did the FBI's Virtual Case File project fail?
options: Requirements were obsolete by the time development finished, with no user feedback during the process
